reply_content:
Henry is right, BUT Henry is also wrong. What he is suggesting is the correct tactic. However, you need the right type of striker/forward to execute what he's suggesting so that it becomes a real and actual threat to the opposing team.

Players (strikers/forwards) with the speed AND goal scoring prowess like Henry, Salah, Eto, Drogba, Mbappe, R9, prime/healthy Pato, prime/healthy Auba... they would ABSOLUTELY WRECK HOUSE against defensive set ups like the one shown here, which Pep LOVES to deploy. 

Managers and squads who figure this out go out and get the strikers and forwards who CAN do this. See Klopp. See JM. See Zizu, See Fat Carlo... 

The truth of the matter is Pep's Barca dominance HEAVILY influenced the style of play- all the way down to park games and the academies. Suddenly, the best players WANTED to be midfielders, not strikers. And coaches up and down the youth levels were stressing possession football and playing out from the back in order to hold that possession. This type of thinking and coaching KIL LED striker development, coaching and more importantly, instinct- even in the players assigned the position.  This is why you see these massive lapses in on field judgements by a whole leagues of teams at the striker position.  You have professional football players aged 22,23,24,25,26 who have never seen nor been coached and trained up to do EXACTLY what Henry is pointing out here in this video

🎉🎉🎉🎉🎉
